metric_value: Implementing data mining is approximately 75% data preparation

metric_value: Professional services can represent as much as 60% of CRM contract

metric_value: Data warehouses can take well over a year to implement

metric_name: NT as data mart platform shift
metric_value: NT is now considered data mart platform-of-choice; suppliers formerly Unix-focused have tuned for NT; NT has driven down data mart prices
notes: Microsoft influence has changed go-to-market strategies and channels of most DSS suppliers

metric_value: Microsoft Plato acquisition from Panorama will increase consolidation trend; many suppliers embracing Plato and planning partnerships despite uncertainty about what Plato actually is
notes: Prediction: Plato's market entry will be catalyst for consolidation before it even ships

metric_value: SQL Server 7.0 OLAP Services (1999) did indeed catalyze market consolidation; several pure-play OLAP vendors declined or were acquired
notes: Outcome: SQL Server 7.0 OLAP Services (1999) did indeed catalyze market consolidation; several pure-play OLAP vendors declined or were acquired

metric_value: Start-ups without true differentiation will fall on road to IPO or be acquired by predominant suppliers; consolidation becoming more common
notes: Prediction for near-term industry consolidation wave

metric_value: Arbor Software and Hyperion Software announced intentions to merge into Hyperion Solutions; targeting leadership in custom on-demand and packaged analytic applications
notes: Merger imminent at time of writing; creates new leader in analytical applications across corporations government and education

metric_value: IBM holds top-ranking position in Data Knowledge marketplace based on combined installed base in hardware software and services; DecisionEdge CRM for finance and insurance
notes: IBM described as single-source supplier for databases transformation tools and analytical/data mining software

metric_value: Reigning monarch in decision support; WorldMark 4700 handles up to 600GB; 5150 for larger; 128-node scalability; prices now compare favorably to Unix competitors
notes: NCR among early leaders in TPC-D benchmarks; Teradata Release 3 with OLAP and data mining extensions planned for late 1998

metric_value: Cognos has installed more than 700000 seats of Impromptu and PowerPlay; tight resource-sharing linkage between client-server and web versions
notes: Cognos Accelerator for SAP R/3 OEMed from Acta Technologies for ETL and data mart population

metric_value: SAS Enterprise Miner has supplanted many third-party suppliers' data mining environments of choice in CRM systems; SEMMA methodology considered leading approach
notes: SAS recognized by other leading suppliers as the data mining standard for CRM implementations

metric_value: Approximately 500 installed sites; selected as tool of choice among information providers; DSS Broadcaster narrows information to web/email/pager subscribers
notes: Recently became public at time of writing; DSS Server tuned for very large database technology

metric_value: Aberdeen believes more integrated CIS-DSS solutions will be introduced in next 12-24 months
notes: CIS (telemarketing telesales SFA customer support) integration with DSS still nascent in 1998

metric_value: Most Data Knowledge tools analyze summary data in offline/batch fashion; real-time decision support slowly emerging
notes: Biggest constraint: Data Knowledge applied on data far removed from business logic and processes

metric_value: Integrating ERP and Data Knowledge is at a relatively primitive stage in 1998; suppliers cannot view ERP as just another data source
notes: ERP changing dramatically; functional domain convergence; vertical-industry specialization required from DSS suppliers

metric_value: Executives view quick-install data marts (weeks) more favorably than data warehouses or slow 3-6 month data marts
notes: Shift: data warehouse returning to cross-corporate analytical hub and feeding downstream data marts rather than direct analysis

metric_value: Sophisticated Global 1000 organizations have spent past few years building Data Knowledge infrastructure and now looking for applications that leverage it
notes: Creates market for OLAP tools and quick-build data marts as second phase of investment

metric_value: Big-ticket CRM systems and data warehouses affecting entire organization increasingly purchased by Chief Financial Officer
notes: LOB or IS division may purchase smaller tools (MDB query/reporting data marts); CFO owns enterprise-wide Data Knowledge
